title: Add Contact Us page, Gallery with admin photo upload, and updated Services list

requirements.summary: Create a Contact Us page displaying company email, phone, and physical address with consistent styling and navigation access
acceptanceCriteria: A Contact Us page is accessible via navigation
The page displays email, phone number, and location/address
The page is styled consistently with the rest of the site
The page is reachable from the bottom navigation or header

file_operations.description: Create Contact Us page that fetches contact info from backend using getContactInfo() and displays email, phone, and location. Include a hero section with title, contact details cards with icons, and optional embedded map for location. Style consistently with existing pages using Tailwind classes. Use PullToRefreshContainer for mobile UX.

Create hook that exports useGetContactInfo (fetches contact info via actor.getContactInfo()) and useUpdateContactInfo mutation (admin-only, calls actor.updateContactInfo with email, phone, location). Include proper loading states and query invalidation on update.

Add a new route for the Contact Us page at /contact with the Route component. Import ContactUsPage and register the route within the router configuration alongside existing customer pages (home, services, gallery, reviews).

Add a Contact navigation item to the bottom navigation bar. Include an appropriate icon (e.g., Mail or MapPin from lucide-react) and link to /contact. Position it alongside existing nav items (Home, Services, Gallery, Reviews, Chat). No authentication required for this item.

Ensure the Contact Us page route (/contact) is included in the customer pages list so that the bottom navigation displays correctly when users navigate to the Contact page. Verify that the layout renders the BottomNav component for this route.

requirements.summary: Add admin photo upload button to Project Gallery page visible only to authenticated admins
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Upload Photo' button is visible only to authenticated admins on the gallery page
Admins can select and upload an image file which then appears in the gallery
Non-admin users do not see the upload button
Upload progress is shown during photo upload
Newly uploaded photos appear in the gallery without a full page reload

file_operations.description: Add an 'Upload Photo' button at the top of the gallery that is conditionally rendered only when useIsAdmin() returns true. Use the authorization component to check admin status. When clicked, open a dialog/modal with a form for uploading a new project: image file input (with upload progress tracking using ExternalBlob.withUploadProgress), title, description, and category selection. On submit, call useAddProject mutation and invalidate project queries to refresh the gallery. Use the blob-storage component for image uploads by converting File to ExternalBlob. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Update the Services page to accurately list all four service categories with descriptions, features, and call-to-action buttons
acceptanceCriteria: Services page lists all relevant service categories with accurate descriptions
Each service card includes a title, description, feature list, and a call-to-action button
The page is styled consistently with the site theme

file_operations.description: Update the service cards to ensure all four categories are present: Digital Printing, Flex & Banner, Offset Printing, and Creative Design. For each service, provide an accurate and detailed description reflecting Nellore Print Hub's actual offerings. Include a feature list (3-5 bullet points per service) highlighting key capabilities. Ensure each card has a consistent layout with a title, description, feature list, and a 'Request Quote' or 'Learn More' button. Use icons from generated assets (frontend/public/assets/generated/digital-icon.dim_256x256.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/flex-icon.dim_256x256.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/offset-icon.dim_256x256.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/design-icon.dim_256x256.png). Keep styling consistent with existing Tailwind classes and mobile-first responsive design.
